div1.entryFree: Suppose, subst. supposition, presumption, opinion: have by marriage made thy daughter mine, while counterfeit --s bleared thine eye, Shr. V, 1, 120. we come short of our s. so far that after seven years' siege yet Troy walls stand, Troil. I, 3, 11. lose not so noble a friend on vain s. Tit. I, 440.
